description: Add full-file diff view mode — shows complete old and new files side by side with changes highlighted.

userStories.title: IPC: Fetch Old File Content at Parent Commit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fetch the old version of a file (from the parent commit) so we can display it alongside the new version.
acceptanceCriteria: Add showFileAtParent(repoPath, commitHash, filePath) method to git-service.ts
It runs: git show PARENT_HASH:PATH where PARENT_HASH is the first parent of commitHash
To get the parent hash: git rev-parse COMMIT_HASH^1
If the file is new (added), return empty string for old content
If the file is deleted, the new content (from showFileAtCommit) will be empty
Register IPC handler in git-ipc.ts: 'git:showFileAtParent'
Expose in preload/index.ts: electronAPI.git.showFileAtParent(repoPath, hash, filePath)
Add type declaration in useLayoutState.ts Window.electronAPI
Binary file detection: if content contains null bytes, return { binary: true }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same pattern as the existing showFileAtCommit: git-service.ts method -> git-ipc.ts handler -> preload expose -> type declaration. The parent hash comes from git rev-parse HASH^1. For merge commits with multiple parents, use the first parent.

userStories.title: Full Diff View Mode — Side-by-Side Complete Files with Highlights
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'Full Diff' view that shows the complete old and new files side by side with changes highlighted in red/green.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a third view mode toggle button in the center diff header bar: 'Diff' | 'Full' | 'File'
Diff = existing hunk-based diff view (unchanged)
File = existing raw file view (unchanged)
Full = new view: two panes side by side showing complete old file (left) and new file (right)
In Full mode: fetch old content via showFileAtParent and new content via showFileAtCommit
Lines that are additions (only in new file) are highlighted with green background
Lines that are deletions (only in old file) are highlighted with red background
Lines that are unchanged appear in both panes with no highlight
Use the existing diff hunks data to determine which line ranges are changed — map hunk line numbers to the full file lines
Both panes scroll in sync (reuse the existing scroll sync from side-by-side diff view)
Both panes show line numbers
Syntax highlighting on all lines (not just changed ones)
Loading state while fetching both file versions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The key challenge is mapping diff hunks to full file lines. Parse the diff to find which line numbers in old/new files are changed. Then when rendering the full files, highlight those lines. Can reuse the SideBySideDiffView component as a base — it already has two-pane scroll sync. The difference is it renders ALL lines, not just diff lines. Use the existing DiffHunk data: each hunk has oldStart/oldCount and newStart/newCount plus line-by-line +/- markers.

userStories.title: Full Diff View — Polish and Edge Cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full diff view to handle edge cases and look polished.
acceptanceCriteria: New files (added): left pane shows 'New file' placeholder, right pane shows full content all highlighted green
Deleted files: left pane shows full content all highlighted red, right pane shows 'File deleted' placeholder
Renamed files: header shows 'old/path → new/path', both files displayed normally
Binary files: show 'Binary file — cannot display' in both panes
Large files (>5000 lines): show 'Large file — click to load' placeholder, load on click
The OLD/NEW pane headers are sticky at the top (position: sticky, top: 0)
Pane headers show the file path and line count
The view mode preference (Diff/Full/File) persists — update the existing diffViewMode in LayoutState to support 'inline' | 'side-by-side' | 'full' | 'file'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update DiffViewMode type in useLayoutState.ts to include 'full' and 'file'. The mode toggle buttons in DiffViewer need to show 4 options now. The RepoView center diff area already handles 'file' mode separately — add 'full' mode alongside it. Edge cases: for new files, git show PARENT^1:path will fail — catch the error and treat as empty content.
